title: Investigate and fix quilting pillow scale mismatch vs Blender
description: The quilting output from tool_quilt.dsl on a plain cube with subdivide_mesh shows visible quilting pillows, but the pillow scale does not match Blender's output at the same parameters (scale_x=0.3, scale_y=0.3, depth=0.05, rotation=75deg). The double subdivision and spherize have already been removed. The remaining scale mismatch has a different root cause that needs investigation. Possible causes: CubeMeshNode vertex positions differ from Blender's default cube (-1,-1,-1 to 1,1,1 vs -0.5 to 0.5), tangent frame computation differences, float_curve control point interpolation differences, or SubdivideMesh producing a different vertex layout than Blender's GeometryNodeSubdivideMesh.
definition-of-done: 1. Root cause of the scale mismatch is identified and documented. 2. The quilting output on a cube at default parameters visually matches Blender's output (pillow count, pillow size, trench depth proportional to mesh size). 3. A mesh fingerprint or vertex count comparison test confirms parity.
testing-plan: 1. Export the Blender quilting result at matching parameters as OBJ, compute mesh fingerprint. 2. Run tool_quilt.dsl at the same parameters, compute mesh fingerprint. 3. Compare vertex counts, bounding box dimensions, and pillow structure. 4. Add a regression test with the expected vertex count or fingerprint.
todos: Compare CubeMeshNode vertex positions with Blender's default cube
Compare SubdivideMeshNode output vertex layout with Blender's SubdivideMesh
Compare tangent frame computation on flat faces vs Blender's implementation
Compare float_curve interpolation with Blender's Float Curve node
unknowns: Whether the mismatch is in the base mesh geometry, the subdivision vertex layout, the tangent frame, or the curve interpolation
Whether Blender's SubdivideMesh produces a different vertex ordering than our implementation
